title: athyrium eremicola
intro: Athyrium eremicola, also known as the spinulose woodsia or desert lady fern, is a species of fern that is native to the southwestern United States and northern Mexico. It has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, which include its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, making it effective in treating wounds and skin irritations. The health benefits of Athyrium eremicola include its ability to reduce inflammation, soothe skin conditions, and promote wound healing. Herbal preparations of Athyrium eremicola typically involve drying and crushing the plant's leaves and stems, which can then be used to make teas, infusions, or salves to harness its medicinal properties.
